_description: Maps Cipher-tube Palette pillar controls to WCAG 2.2 Level AA success criteria. Each criterion identifies the implementing component, automated testing method, and current conformance status.

criteria.title: Non-text Content
criteria.description: All non-text content has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette component library requires alt text on all images, icons, and media. Design-token system includes aria-label mappings. axe-core scans enforce presence of text alternatives.

criteria.title: Info and Relationships
criteria.description: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette enforces semantic HTML structure. Headings, lists, tables, and form labels use appropriate elements. ARIA landmarks used for page regions.
criteria.automated_testing: axe-core rules: heading-order, list, definition-list, table-fake-caption

criteria.title: Orientation
criteria.description: Content does not restrict its view and operation to a single display orientation unless essential.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette CSS uses responsive layouts without orientation locks. Playwright cross-browser tests validate portrait and landscape rendering.

criteria.title: Identify Input Purpose
criteria.description: The purpose of each input field collecting user information can be programmatically determined.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette form components include autocomplete attributes per HTML 5.2 input purposes. Design tokens define standard input-purpose mappings.

criteria.title: Use of Color
criteria.description: Color is not used as the only visual means of conveying information.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette design tokens include secondary indicators (icons, patterns, text labels) for all color-dependent states. Storybook a11y addon validates.

criteria.title: Contrast (Minimum)
criteria.description: Text and images of text have a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 (3:1 for large text).
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Design-token color system enforces minimum contrast ratios. Token validation script checks contrastRatio field on all color tokens. axe-core and Lighthouse audit contrast.
criteria.automated_testing: axe-core rule: color-contrast; Lighthouse accessibility audit; token validation script
criteria.notes: contrastRatio field required on all color tokens — enforced by palette-a11y-check.yml

criteria.title: Resize Text
criteria.description: Text can be resized up to 200% without loss of content or functionality.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette typography tokens use rem units exclusively. Layout uses CSS Grid/Flexbox with relative sizing. Token validation rejects non-rem font-size values.
criteria.automated_testing: Token validation script (rem enforcement); Playwright zoom tests
criteria.notes: rem enforcement validated by palette-a11y-check.yml design token validation

criteria.title: Non-text Contrast
criteria.description: UI components and graphical objects have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent colors.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Design tokens define border, focus-ring, and icon colors with minimum 3:1 contrast. axe-core scans validate interactive component contrast.

criteria.title: Content on Hover or Focus
criteria.description: Additional content triggered by hover or focus is dismissible, hoverable, and persistent.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette tooltip and popover components implement dismissible (Escape key), hoverable (pointer can move to content), and persistent (remains until dismissed) behaviors.

criteria.title: Keyboard
criteria.description: All functionality is operable through a keyboard interface.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette component library ensures all interactive elements are keyboard-operable. Tab order follows logical reading order. Custom widgets implement ARIA keyboard patterns.
criteria.automated_testing: axe-core rule: keyboard; pa11y keyboard navigation; Playwright keyboard tests

criteria.title: No Keyboard Trap
criteria.description: Keyboard focus can be moved away from any component using standard navigation.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette modal and dialog components implement focus trapping with Escape key exit. No component traps focus without an exit mechanism.
criteria.automated_testing: axe-core rule: no-trap; Playwright focus tests

criteria.title: Bypass Blocks
criteria.description: A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content repeated on multiple pages.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette layout includes skip-to-main-content link. ARIA landmark roles mark navigation, main, and complementary regions.

criteria.title: Focus Visible
criteria.description: Any keyboard-operable UI has a visible keyboard focus indicator.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette design tokens define focus-ring styles with minimum 3:1 contrast. :focus-visible CSS pseudo-class used for keyboard-only focus indicators. Custom focus rings on all interactive components.

criteria.title: Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
criteria.description: When a UI component receives keyboard focus, it is not entirely hidden by author-created content.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette z-index system ensures focused elements are never fully obscured by overlays, sticky headers, or other positioned content. Scroll-margin applied to focusable elements.

criteria.title: Dragging Movements
criteria.description: Functionality that uses dragging can be operated by a single pointer without dragging, unless dragging is essential.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette drag-and-drop components provide alternative single-click or button-based mechanisms (move up/down buttons, select-and-place). Keyboard arrow-key reordering available.

criteria.title: Target Size (Minimum)
criteria.description: Targets for pointer inputs are at least 24x24 CSS pixels, with exceptions.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette design tokens define minimum interactive target sizes (44x44 CSS px for mobile, 24x24 minimum for desktop). Component library enforces minimum tap targets.
criteria.automated_testing: Lighthouse target-size audit; custom CSS lint rule
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.2. Palette exceeds minimum with 44x44 on mobile.

criteria.title: Language of Page
criteria.description: The default human language of each page can be programmatically determined.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette layout component sets lang attribute on the root HTML element. Theming engine supports RTL/LTR based on language direction.

criteria.title: Consistent Help
criteria.description: If a page contains help mechanisms, they occur in the same relative order across pages.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette navigation component places help link in consistent position across all views. Help mechanism included in standard layout template.

criteria.title: Error Identification
criteria.description: If an input error is automatically detected, the item in error is identified and described to the user in text.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette form components display inline error messages associated with aria-describedby. Error states use both color and icon indicators.
criteria.automated_testing: axe-core rule: aria-valid-attr-value; Playwright form tests

criteria.title: Redundant Entry
criteria.description: Information previously entered by or provided to the user that is required to be entered again is either auto-populated or available for the user to select.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette form components implement auto-fill and session-based data persistence for multi-step flows. Previously entered values available via select/autocomplete.
criteria.automated_testing: Playwright multi-step form tests; manual review

criteria.title: Accessible Authentication (Minimum)
criteria.description: A cognitive function test is not required for any step in an authentication process unless an alternative is provided.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette authentication flows support password managers (no copy-paste blocking), WebAuthn/passkeys, and do not require CAPTCHA as the sole authentication method.
criteria.automated_testing: Manual review; Playwright authentication flow tests

criteria.title: Name, Role, Value
criteria.description: For all UI components, the name and role can be programmatically determined; states, properties, and values can be programmatically set.
criteria.ciphertubeimplementation: Palette component library uses semantic HTML elements with ARIA attributes where custom widgets are needed. All interactive components expose accessible name, role, and state.

automated_tools.wcag_coverage: WCAG 2.0 A/AA, WCAG 2.1 A/AA, WCAG 2.2 AA (partial)

automated_tools.scope: Cross-browser keyboard, focus, and interaction testing

testing_infrastructure.manualreviewscope: All WCAG 2.2 AA criteria not fully covered by automated tools, including cognitive and contextual criteria (3.2.6, 3.3.7, 3.3.8, 2.5.7, 2.5.8, 2.4.11)
